expectations.text: Response includes a call or description of calling start_proxy to start the proxy
expectations.evidence: Step 1 explicitly calls start_proxy() and shows the expected return shape with proxyUrl and caCertPath

expectations.text: Response mentions installing or trusting the CA certificate (caCertPath / ca.pem)
expectations.evidence: Section B 'Install the CA certificate on your device' with full instructions for installing ca.pem

expectations.text: Response includes Android-specific setup: either network_security_config.xml or mention of certificate trust for API 24+
expectations.evidence: Includes full network_security_config.xml snippet with <debug-overrides><trust-anchors><certificates src="user" /></trust-anchors></debug-overrides> and AndroidManifest.xml reference

expectations.text: Response tells the user to configure their device/WiFi proxy to point at the returned proxy URL
expectations.evidence: Section A 'Configure your Android device to use the proxy' with manual WiFi proxy instructions including hostname and port 8080

expectations.text: Response asks the user to trigger their app traffic and indicates what to do next (inspect), rather than stopping at setup
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'Go ahead and use your app â€” tap through the flows you want to inspect. Then let me know and I'll pull up what was captured.'
